hyperedges.label: Assignee Management Flow (ordering, editor, quick-assign, members)
Session Resilience: error classification → banner → confirm probe → host-owned health
Multi-Select → Bulk Actions → Combined Window Flow
Server reachability + auth failure handling evolution
Palette → Merge Requests → My Work Dashboard feature sequence
Session/server health evolution: webview auth watch → unavailable taxonomy → host-owned authority
Electrobun native window machinery (per-issue singleton, combined pager, settings singleton)

nodes.label:
{
  selection,
  toggleSelectMode,
  onAddLabels,
  onRemoveLabels,
  onSetAssignee,
  onSetStatus,
  onOpenCombined,
}
nodes.norm_label:
{
  selection,
  toggleselectmode,
  onaddlabels,
  onremovelabels,
  onsetassignee,
  onsetstatus,
  onopencombined,
}

nodes.label:
{
  dragging,
  draggingIid,
  dragOverKey,
  justDropped,
  onDragStart,
  clearDrag,
  onDrop,
  isDropTarget,
  ghostIndex,
}
nodes.norm_label:
{
  dragging,
  draggingiid,
  dragoverkey,
  justdropped,
  ondragstart,
  cleardrag,
  ondrop,
  isdroptarget,
  ghostindex,
}

nodes.label: Refined-Dark Linear-Class Aesthetic
nodes.rationale: Dark theme chosen for the focused, editor-adjacent viewing context of a solo developer doing backlog triage; near-black cool-slate (hue 256) surfaces; color reserved for meaning (labels/status/priority) not decoration; one rare amber accent at ~10% visual weight; explicitly avoids the cyan/purple-on-dark AI look, side-stripe accents, gradient text, and glassmorphism.

nodes.label: Fixed-Rem Type Scale Tokens
nodes.rationale: Fixed rem sizes, never fluid clamp(): a dense instrument UI wants spatial predictability. Every size is a named token (text-micro through text-hero); the text-title token exists to unify the page h1 which previously rendered 26/30/32px across three views. Hanken Grotesk for UI, Geist Mono for tabular ids; self-hosted via @fontsource so they work offline behind the internal CA.

nodes.label: Signal Lamp Liveness Readout
nodes.rationale: Steady amber when idle, breathes only while a query is in flight (driven by useIsFetching) — a loading affordance kin to spinners, the sole sanctioned perpetual-motion exception to the quiet-motion principle.

nodes.label: Odometer Hero Count Roll
nodes.rationale: Issue/project counts resolve digit by digit, roll direction tracks the value; inherits host font/color, carries sr-only plain value, snaps under reduced motion. Supersedes the old .animate-count rise+fade.

nodes.label: View Transition Continuity (withViewTransition)
nodes.rationale: Navigation reads as one instrument retuning, not a page swap. All View Transitions go through withViewTransition() which degrades to an instant update where VT is unavailable or motion is reduced; row⇄board-card morphs are capped at 30 shared names so a large set never snapshots hundreds of nodes.

nodes.label: Scoped Label Parsing (scope::value)
nodes.rationale: Lift signal out of noise: parse scoped GitLab labels, promote priority/type/status into glyphs and chips, render the rest as two-tone pills with luminance-derived readable text.

nodes.label: Toast System (useToast + ToastHost)
nodes.rationale: Restrained bottom-right stack in the instrument idiom — solid bg-card focal cards, no glassmorphism, tone-specific auto-dismiss (failures linger 10s). Module-singleton state like useConfirm. Two-channel pipeline alerts: in-app toast always, OS notification only when the app isn't active so the user is never double-pinged.

nodes.label: Armed Pipeline Bell Breathing
nodes.rationale: Subscribing to a running pipeline makes its bell breathe amber — the liveness-lamp idiom applied to a subscription; breath onset is the arm confirmation and stops the instant the run leaves flight. Sanctioned sibling of the header lamp's breathing exception.

nodes.label: Issues Workspace (List/Board + Click-to-Filter)
nodes.rationale: Not GitLab-shaped: List⇄Board toggle with kanban workflow-status columns; any facet on a row/card toggles a filter. Every facet reduces to a label or assignee filter so the GraphQL query needs no new variables; issues infinite-load at 50/page so client-side sort/group sees the whole set (priority and status live in labels, which the server can't order by).

nodes.label: Lumen (desktop GitLab issue tracker)
nodes.rationale: GitLab's web UI is heavy for the day-to-day triage loop; Lumen is a focused native shell over the GitLab GraphQL+REST APIs that opens fast, keeps a warm on-disk cache, and stays usable when the server hiccups. Pre-release 0.1.0, single-user, targets self-hosted GitLab behind internal CAs.

nodes.label: Two-Process Host/Webview Architecture
nodes.rationale: Webview (Vue 3 SPA) and Bun host talk over a typed RPC bridge. The token lives only in the host — read fresh from config.json (mode 0600) on every request and attached host-side, so the webview never sees it and a swapped token takes effect on the next request with no restart.

nodes.label: GitLab Error Kind Union (auth/unavailable/graphql/network)
nodes.rationale: Errors normalize to a small union: 401/403→auth, 5xx and transport failures→unavailable (host returns a 503 sentinel when fetch throws), 404/other→network — so an unreachable server is never confused with a bad token.

nodes.label: graphql-request Transport Swapped for RPC
nodes.rationale: Keeps graphql-request's query construction and ClientError semantics but routes transport through an RPC round-trip to the host, preserving the upstream HTTP status so errors classify correctly.

nodes.label: Live-Instance GraphQL Codegen Workflow
nodes.rationale: Typed documents are generated into the gitignored src/gitlab/generated/ by introspecting the live instance; TypeScript shows errors against generated types until the user runs bun codegen after changing any query.

nodes.label: macOS Code Signing & Notarization (bun run dist)
nodes.rationale: Dev builds are never signed (Electrobun can't codesign the dev launcher), so downloaded copies hit Gatekeeper's 'damaged' wall. Self-signed identity downgrades it to 'unidentified developer'; only Apple Developer ID + notarization fully clears it. Opt-in via env vars read by electrobun.config.ts.
nodes.norm_label: macos code signing & notarization (bun run dist)

nodes.label: Session Resilience: server-unavailable vs token-invalid
nodes.rationale: An unreachable server must never wrongly prompt for a new token; the blocking re-connect overlay fires only for genuine auth failures, and session state enforces an auth-always-wins invariant in its setters.

nodes.label: ConnectionBanner Self-Healing Recovery Poll
nodes.rationale: Non-blocking 'Can't reach GitLab — retrying…' banner with 2s→5s→15s backoff; the probe hits a cheap health query directly, bypassing vue-query to avoid a re-trigger loop; clears and refetches on recovery, escalates to the re-connect overlay only if the probe returns 401/403.

nodes.label: Eager Route Imports (no per-route code-splitting)
nodes.rationale: Dynamically-imported route chunks are flaky to fetch over the views:// scheme ('Importing a module script failed'). A local desktop app pays nothing for eager imports, and they remove the whole class of chunk-load failures.

nodes.label: getInitialRoute RPC for Popout Windows
nodes.rationale: Popout windows came up blank under views:// because the route rode in the initial URL fragment, which the scheme can't load. Popouts now load the bare app and receive their route from the host via RPC, applied client-side before the router mounts — no fragment, no flash. URL builders became route builders (issueWindowRoute / issuesWindowRoute).

nodes.label: installAuthWatch Confirmation Probe
nodes.rationale: A single transient 401 or forbidden sub-resource no longer logs the user out: one authoritative probe confirms before latching the blocking overlay — only a probe that also fails auth latches; a clean probe proves the token valid; an unreachable probe shows the banner instead. probeServer lives in useSession, shared with the recovery poll.

nodes.label: Opt-in Code Signing Wired into electrobun.config.ts
nodes.rationale: Driven by env vars so the default build stays unsigned; bun run sign-cert mints a self-signed identity for local builds; Apple Developer ID + notarization turn on when Apple creds are present. Addresses the Gatekeeper 'is damaged' error on downloaded copies.
nodes.norm_label: opt-in code signing wired into electrobun.config.ts

nodes.label: Inline Dark Background Pre-Style
nodes.rationale: An inline html background of oklch(0.178 0.008 256) avoids a white flash before the stylesheet loads — the exact design-system background token.

nodes.label: In-App MCP Server
nodes.rationale: Off by default; enabled via an mcp block in the app config file; listens on 127.0.0.1:7437 streamable HTTP and requires a bearer token on every request.

nodes.label: MCP GitLab Tool Set (14 tools)
nodes.rationale: lumen_issues_list/get/create/update/comment, lumen_mrs_list/get/comment/review, lumen_labels_list, lumen_milestones_list, lumen_me, lumen_members_list, lumen_search — project paths are full group/project paths.

nodes.label: GitLab Issue Field GraphQL Shapes (live-instance confirmed)
nodes.rationale: This note exists because the introspected SDL (src/gitlab/schema.graphql) is gitignored, not committed; __type(name:) introspection is disabled on the instance so lookups go through __schema { types } filtered client-side.
nodes.norm_label: gitlab issue field graphql shapes (live-instance confirmed)

nodes.label: EE Field Writes via updateIssue (no codegen)
nodes.rationale: Health status, Locked, and Parent Epic are cheap: read via the raw-string useIssue query, write via fields that already exist on the generated UpdateIssueInput — no codegen rerun required. Assignees are explicitly NOT on UpdateIssueInput; they use the dedicated issueSetAssignees mutation.
nodes.norm_label: ee field writes via updateissue (no codegen)

nodes.label: Iteration via workItemUpdate Widget Path
nodes.rationale: UpdateIssueInput has no iteration field, so Iteration must go through the work-item widget path (workItemUpdate with iterationWidget), the same raw-string pattern as the native Status field in useWorkItemStatus.ts; reserve the work-item path for what updateIssue lacks.

nodes.label: Host-Owned Server Health (src/bun/serverHealth.ts)
nodes.rationale: Server-reachability is host-owned: one recovery loop broadcast to every window so all banners show the same countdown ticking in lockstep (they previously drifted per-window); Retry-now in one window flips all banners; cross-window behavior isn't unit-testable (Electrobun runtime) so it is verified by a manual two-window smoke.

nodes.label: Route-Driven Issue Drawer (?issue query)
nodes.rationale: A ?issue=<iid> query param on the issues route drives the drawer so back/refresh/links all work; IssueDrawer is pure UI (emits update:open and expand) while IssueList owns all routing — close strips the param via router.replace so closing adds no history entry; the drawer body reuses IssueDetail.vue.

nodes.label: Lumen MVP Implementation Plan
nodes.rationale: Four MVP slices: project pick → filtered issue list → issue detail with notes → mutations. GitLab is the source of truth (no local DB); Vue Query owns caching; pure mapping/error logic is isolated and unit-tested without network.

nodes.label: normalizeError (GitLabError normalization)
nodes.rationale: Pure helper mapping graphql-request ClientError/network errors into a typed GitLabError union (originally auth/graphql/network/unknown, later extended with unavailable).

nodes.label: issueParams (filter mapping + query keys)
nodes.rationale: Pure IssueFilters type, stable query-key builders (issuesKey/issueKey), and toIssuesVars filter→GraphQL-variable mapping that omits empty filters and drops state=all.

nodes.label: withQuery Vue Query Test Harness
nodes.rationale: Mounts a composable inside a Vue Query provider with retries off so failures surface immediately; used by every composable test across subsequent plans.

nodes.label: Codegen as the API Contract Test
nodes.rationale: Codegen validates every graphql() document against the live instance schema — it IS the contract test for API shape. The client-preset graphql() tag only types documents codegen has already seen, so codegen must rerun before typecheck/build whenever a document changes; Vitest does not typecheck, so unit tests run without it.
nodes.norm_label: codegen as the api contract test

nodes.label: useScratchpad (local-only per-issue notes)
nodes.rationale: Wraps useLocalStorage with a reactive getter key (lumen:scratchpad:fullPath#iid) so the ref re-keys when the viewed issue changes; notes never reach GitLab. Rendered once in IssueDetail so the drawer gets it for free since IssueDrawer embeds IssueDetail.

nodes.label: orderAssignees (relationship-grouped people ordering)
nodes.rationale: Pure helper ordering project-related people into relationship groups (originator → current assignees → note authors → other members), deduping each person to their highest-priority group while flagging current assignees with isAssigned.

nodes.label: Assignee Editor + Quick Assign Split Plan
nodes.norm_label: assignee editor + quick assign split plan

nodes.label: assigneeSections (shared grouped assignee view)
nodes.rationale: Derives current assignees plus relationship-grouped labelled sections (Reporter/Assigned/Commented/Project members) by reusing orderAssignees, so the assignee editor and quick-assign share one ordering; accepts the issue structurally to stay free of generated types.

nodes.label: AssigneeEditor Component
nodes.rationale: Removable full-name rows plus an additive add/remove dropdown; owns its useSetAssignees mutation but has no error UI — failures bubble up to IssueDetail's single ErrorNotice via an error emit.

nodes.label: QuickAssign (pick-one-replace button)
nodes.rationale: Simplified to a labelled button whose grouped dropdown replaces all assignees with the clicked person; granular add/remove moved to AssigneeEditor.

nodes.label: Issue Editing, Filtering & Editable Tags Plan
nodes.norm_label: issue editing, filtering & editable tags plan

nodes.label: useIssueDraft (buffered Save/Cancel edit)
nodes.rationale: Pure edit logic in lib/issueEdit.ts; the composable buffers changes and orchestrates only the mutations the diff requires on Save. The draft re-syncs from the server only while clean, so a background refetch never clobbers in-flight edits.

nodes.label: useIssueFilters (URL-persisted filter state)
nodes.rationale: Filter state round-trips to the route query and drives the shared useIssues query for list and board; adds author and assigneeWildcardId NONE (the Unassigned sentinel) mappings.

nodes.label: useConfirm (promise-based confirm singleton)
nodes.rationale: A promise-based confirm singleton plus a ConfirmDialog mounted once in App backs the dirty-guard on unsaved edits; later reused as the pattern template for the settings dialog and bulk-action gating.

nodes.label: Comments-in-Save, Grouped Label Menus, Collapsible Scratchpad Plan
nodes.norm_label: comments-in-save, grouped label menus, collapsible scratchpad plan

nodes.label: labelGroups (groupLabelsByScope + toggleScoped)
nodes.rationale: Pure, framework-free scope grouping (preferred scopes first, unscoped Other last) plus toggleScoped: selecting a scoped value removes any other selected title in the same scope (scoped-label exclusivity), while unscoped labels are plain toggles. Rendered by a shared LabelGroupMenu consumed by LabelPicker (exclusive) and IssueFilterPanel (plain multi).

nodes.label: Comment Folded into the Issue Draft
nodes.rationale: The pending comment becomes a field of the useIssueDraft orchestration (not the pure IssueDraft): it marks the draft dirty, posts on Save, clears only on full success, and a failed note post leaves comment + dirty intact so the user can retry or cancel. The standalone Comment button is removed.
nodes.norm_label: comment folded into the issue draft

nodes.label: Collapsible Scratchpad with Content Marker
nodes.rationale: Collapsed by default with a dot marker when it has content; open state persisted per issue (lumen:scratchpad-open:fullPath#iid) mirroring the note's per-issue keying; v-show keeps the textarea in the DOM so existing tests still pass.

nodes.label: IssueComposer Slide-Over
nodes.rationale: Replaces the persistent quick-create bar with an on-demand right-side slide-over built on the existing Sheet (mirroring IssueDrawer); creates an issue with title, description, labels, and assignee in one mutation; opens from a header button or the C key; the new issue gets a flash-highlight in the list.

nodes.label: useProjectMembers Query
nodes.rationale: The assignee picker needs project members and the app had no users query — previously assignees were only known from already-loaded issues; mirrors useProjectLabels exactly.

nodes.label: EditableField (rendered/edit toggle wrapper)
nodes.rationale: A small reusable wrapper owning the rendered/edit toggle (v-model:editing + view/edit slots); IssueDetail uses one per field, defaulting to rendered and collapsing back to rendered after a successful Save — layered on top of the existing buffered Save/Cancel model.

nodes.label: Issue Media — All Media Types + Popout Viewer Plan
nodes.norm_label: issue media — all media types + popout viewer plan

nodes.label: classifyUpload + extractMedia (extension-based media classifier)
nodes.rationale: A shared extension-based classifier in lib/markdown.ts decides how each upload renders (image/video/audio/file), case-insensitively, ignoring query/hash and treating extensionless paths as files; reused by the pure extractMedia() that feeds the viewer.

nodes.label: MediaViewer Lightbox
nodes.rationale: A reka-ui Dialog fullscreen lightbox paging through all images/videos in an issue; useIssueMedia assembles description + comment media into a source-tagged collection; IssueDetail wires it via a gallery button and a delegated click on inline triggers.

nodes.label: URL as Single Source of Truth for View State
nodes.rationale: Sort/group/view/scope are promoted into the URL query alongside filter keys so useIssueFilters owns all list view-state; localStorage (keyed per project by fullPath) only seeds the URL on arrival when no filter key is present and mirrors it on every change — the list returns exactly as the user left it per project.
nodes.norm_label: url as single source of truth for view state

nodes.label: LumenRPC Typed Contract (rpcContract.ts)
nodes.rationale: Single source of truth for the RPC surface (gitlabGraphql, gitlabRest, gitlabAsset, config CRUD), imported by both Bun handlers and the view wrapper. Electrobun's own view-side generics are loose, so app code stays typed by funneling every call through one typed wrapper that internally casts to any — do not fight the framework generics.

nodes.label: RPC Fetch Shim for graphql-request
nodes.rationale: src/gitlab/client.ts keeps graphql-request but swaps its transport for the gitlabGraphql RPC, replacing the old /gitlab Vite proxy; images load as blob URLs fetched through gitlabAsset RPC.

nodes.label: Disk-Persisted vue-query Cache
nodes.rationale: A persisted vue-query cache (query-persist-client-core, lib/persist.ts with persister + buster) gives instant-offline render on launch.

nodes.label: App-Support Host Config (src/bun/config.ts)
nodes.rationale: The Bun main process reads the token from an app-support config file with first-run env import; the token never enters the webview — handlers attach it host-side with TLS verification relaxed for internal CAs.

nodes.label: SettingsDialog Singleton (Connection/About/Cache)
nodes.rationale: A singleton dialog mounted once in App.vue, driven by a module-level reactive store (useSettings) — deliberately mirroring the existing ConfirmDialog + useConfirm pattern; the native app menu (⌘,) dispatches a lumen:open-settings window event into the webview via win.webview.executeJavascript.

nodes.label: useGitlabConnect (shared connect + probe)
nodes.rationale: Onboarding's connect/probe logic extracted so both the renamed ConnectView (/connect, formerly the misnamed /settings) and the dialog's Connection section share it; save() performs saveConfig + a currentUser probe and resolves true so the caller decides the success side-effect (navigate vs toast).

nodes.label: Feature-Sliced Source Layout
nodes.rationale: Flat layer directories restructured into features/<domain>/{components,composables,lib} + shared/{ui,components,composables,lib} as a pure git-mv + import-rewrite with no behavior change; a boundary-anchored codemod (scripts/reorg-remap.mjs) rewrites @/ imports only at module-specifier boundaries so useIssue never corrupts useIssues.

nodes.label: Vitest as the Reorg Gate (typecheck expected red)
nodes.rationale: Typecheck is expected red independent of the reorg because src/gitlab/generated is gitignored and only produced by bun codegen against the live instance. The gate is bunx vitest run — the package 'test' script is watch mode and never exits, and bun test runs Bun's built-in runner (no vi, no jsdom) spuriously failing ~227 tests. Secondary gate: bunx vite build for unresolved imports.
nodes.norm_label: vitest as the reorg gate (typecheck expected red)

nodes.label: Issue Multi-Select Plan 1: Selection + Bulk Actions
nodes.norm_label: issue multi-select plan 1: selection + bulk actions

nodes.label: useIssueSelection (provide/inject select mode)
nodes.rationale: An ephemeral composable provided by IssueList and injected by rows/cards, holding select-mode plus a Set of selected iids.

nodes.label: BulkActionBar (floating bulk-action bar)
nodes.rationale: Hosts the pickers and emits apply-intents (and open-combined); IssueList maps intents to the bulk-actions composable rather than the bar mutating directly.

nodes.label: useBulkIssueActions (Promise.allSettled bulk apply)
nodes.rationale: Loops the existing per-issue mutations (useRetagIssue/useReassignIssue/useSetIssueStatus) over the selection via Promise.allSettled, gating on useConfirm and summarizing the outcome via pushToast — reuse over new bulk endpoints.

nodes.label: Issue Multi-Select Plan 2: Combined Multi-Issue Window
nodes.norm_label: issue multi-select plan 2: combined multi-issue window

nodes.label: MultiIssueWindow (pager over IssueDetail)
nodes.rationale: A combined native window showing one issue at a time behind a ‹ X of N › pager, each page fully editable: renders IssueDetail with embedded=true so IssueDetail emits update:dirty for the pager's discard-guard while MultiIssueWindow owns title, pager, and chrome.

nodes.label: issuesWindowUrl + openIssuesWindow RPC
nodes.rationale: A host RPC openIssuesWindow({fullPath, iids}) spawns a fresh BrowserWindow (no dedupe) at a hash route carrying comma-joined iids (order preserved = pager order) and ?window=1; built by issuesWindowUrl with the same base rules as issueWindowUrl. Later superseded by route builders when fragments proved unloadable over views://.

nodes.label: Plan: Open Issues in Native Window
nodes.norm_label: plan: open issues in native window

nodes.label: issueWindowUrl URL builder
nodes.rationale: Pure, testable URL builder mirroring startUrl.ts; route lives in the hash because the app uses createWebHashHistory, and ?window=1 marks a focused single-issue window.

nodes.label: openIssueWindow RPC handler
nodes.rationale: One native window per issue keyed by fullPath#iid so re-expanding focuses the existing window instead of spawning a duplicate; per-window close events keep the registry accurate; windows cascade to avoid perfect overlap.

nodes.label: buildRpc per-window RPC factory
nodes.rationale: Each native window needs its own RPC bridge, so the inline rpc const is refactored into a factory producing a fresh config per window.

nodes.label: Windowed mode (?window=1)
nodes.rationale: The router maps ?window=1 to a windowed prop (rather than useRoute) because tests fully mock vue-router; windowed suppresses the back-to-list arrow in a standalone native window.

nodes.label: sessionState module singleton
nodes.rationale: Module-level reactive flag mirroring the useSettings shared-state pattern; flipped when a data query/mutation fails auth.

nodes.label: installAuthWatch cache watcher
nodes.rationale: Subscribes to vue-query query + mutation caches for kind:'auth' errors. Recovery probes run directly through rpc (not vue-query), so the overlay's re-probe never reaches the watched caches — no re-trigger loop.

nodes.label: SessionExpiredOverlay
nodes.rationale: Full-screen, non-dismissable recovery surface; a clean re-probe earns a full window.location.reload() — the bulletproof restart, since the host holds no token state. Reuses useGitlabConnect for the token field.

nodes.label: Behavior-preserving extraction (move, not rewrite)
nodes.rationale: Each task moves existing code verbatim behind a precise interface with zero behavior/API/visual change, so the existing view tests act as the regression gate and any task is independently revertable.

nodes.label: useRepoPath composable
nodes.rationale: Dedupe of the repo-name/path-prefix emphasis split shared by picker/list/detail headers.

nodes.label: useTabNav composable
nodes.rationale: Tab-style hops between a project's surfaces morph the shared repo title via view transitions; modified clicks fall through to the real href so open-in-new-window still works.

nodes.label: IssueBoard component
nodes.rationale: Board sizing (boardTopEl sentinel + useElementBounding + boardStyle) moved into the component so the measurement lives next to the element it measures.

nodes.label: useSpringCursor composable
nodes.rationale: Spring-rail cursor logic generalized away from projects (count ref + row finder + reset triggers) and placed in shared/.

nodes.label: applyOrder pure helper
nodes.rationale: Re-sequences groups by a stored key order without losing any: unknown order keys ignored, new groups append after in default order; deterministic default ordering in issueView.ts stays untouched.

nodes.label: useGroupOrder localStorage store
nodes.rationale: Per-project, per-dimension custom ordering keyed by the grouping string the list/board already use, so a single entry is shared across both views. Local-only: the columns are synthetic, so GitLab has no order to persist server-side.

nodes.label: useGroupReorder (native HTML5 DnD)
nodes.rationale: Uses a dedicated drag MIME type (application/x-lumen-group) so a group/column reorder is never mistaken for a card drag (card DnD uses text/plain); the two interactions coexist.

nodes.label: reorderToIndex pure helper
nodes.rationale: The pointer-driven reorder thinks in terms of a landing index rather than an over-key, so reorderToIndex replaces (and retires) reorderKeys.

nodes.label: insertionIndexFor reorder geometry
nodes.rationale: Pure geometry turning cursor position + item rects into landing index, insertion-bar offset (in container content coordinates so the bar tracks scrolled content), and a no-op flag; computeInsertion is the thin DOM wrapper.

nodes.label: Pointer-driven useGroupReorder rewrite
nodes.rationale: Replacing native DnD with pointer events enables a cursor-following rich-chip ghost, a live insertion bar, column lift, drop settle, edge auto-scroll, and Esc-to-cancel; the composable owns drag state/geometry/commit and renders nothing — components draw from its state.

nodes.label: unavailable error kind + 503 sentinel
nodes.rationale: The host catches fetch transport throws and returns a 503 sentinel, unifying 'server threw' and 'server returned 5xx' into one unavailable path so normalizeError/httpError need only a single >=500 rule — a down server never triggers a re-auth prompt.

nodes.label: Auth-wins escalation invariant
nodes.rationale: expired and unavailable are mutually exclusive and auth always wins: markSessionExpired clears the banner, markServerUnavailable is a no-op once expired — a token problem must never be downgraded to a transient banner.

nodes.label: useServerRecovery backoff poll
nodes.rationale: 2s → 5s → 15s capped backoff probing the cheap currentUser query directly via rpc (bypassing vue-query so it never re-triggers installAuthWatch); ok clears the banner and refetches everything, auth escalates to the expired overlay.

nodes.label: ConnectionBanner
nodes.rationale: Non-blocking, self-healing toast (role=status, no backdrop) below the expired overlay's z-index; the recovery poll lives with the banner so it runs exactly while the banner is shown.

nodes.label: PaletteContext + Command types
nodes.rationale: Everything a source needs is passed in via PaletteContext so command sources stay pure and testable; GROUP_ORDER is the single source of group ordering.

nodes.label: Pure palette command sources
nodes.rationale: routeCommands / issueJumpCommand / issueCommands / projectCommands / savedViewCommands / filterByQuery as pure builders; project-scoped commands appear only with an open project.

nodes.label: usePaletteIssueSearch
nodes.rationale: Debounced (200ms) current-project title search, gated to >=2 chars and not a bare issue number; a failed search must never break the palette — error/loading collapse to []. Hand-typed GraphQL, no codegen.

nodes.label: usePaletteCommands merge composable
nodes.rationale: Merges sources into grouped + flat command lists so arrow keys walk a single flat list across section headers; empty groups drop out.

nodes.label: railFields descriptor registry
nodes.rationale: A pure field-descriptor registry is the single source of truth for each field's label, pinned-ness, populated-ness, canonical ordering, and clear action; pinned fields (status/labels/assignees) always render and never appear in the Add menu.

nodes.label: useRailFields composable
nodes.rationale: Owns transient per-session revealed/removed sets (never persisted; cleared on save/cancel) and derives visibleKeys + hiddenFields; lives in the parent IssueDetail because that's where the draft lives, keeping the rail child mutation-free.

nodes.label: Rail progressive disclosure
nodes.rationale: Only populated (and pinned) fields show; empty fields hide behind '+ Add field'. A field populated in the last-synced original but cleared in the draft stays visible until save, so clearing a value keeps the field editable pre-save. Pure presentation over the existing draft — no GraphQL changes.

nodes.label: useSavedViews namespace generalization
nodes.rationale: The only shared-code change: parameterize useSavedViews by storage namespace + filter keys so issues and MRs share it without colliding; issue storage key gains an 'issue' segment, resetting existing views (acceptable pre-release).

nodes.label: mrView pure lib (toMrVars, mrStateLabel)
nodes.rationale: Pure MR filter-keys, slice-to-GraphQL-vars mapping, draft tri-state, and display-state precedence (draft over open).

nodes.label: useMrFilters URL slice
nodes.rationale: Mirrors useIssueFilters' URL round-trip + per-project localStorage persistence pattern, with MR keys and no group/view/scope.

nodes.label: useMrAddNote reply mutation
nodes.rationale: discussionId makes the note a thread reply; invalidates the MR detail query so the reply lands on the next refetch.

nodes.label: timeAgo shared util lift
nodes.rationale: Lifted from pipelineFormat into src/shared/lib/time.ts with a re-export so existing importers keep working.

nodes.label: parseIssuePath webPath parser
nodes.rationale: GitLab's root issues query exposes no project object — only webPath like /group/sub/proj/-/issues/42 — so the dashboard parses fullPath+iid to deep-link into the in-app issue route, falling back to an external link when unparseable.

nodes.label: DashboardLane + cross-project rows
nodes.rationale: Three lanes (Assigned Issues, Assigned MRs, Awaiting My Review) with shared MR_NODE_FIELDS selection set kept in one place so the field list can't drift between the assigned and review-requested queries.

nodes.label: MyWork home view + routing change
nodes.rationale: / becomes My Work; the picker moves to /projects but keeps the 'projects' route name so existing consumers work; post-connect redirect lands on home.

nodes.label: AppShell module (rail + top bar + container)
nodes.rationale: Persistent shell foundation: slim icon rail (AppIconRail) + contextual top bar (AppTopBar) + ViewContainer, composed by AppShell around RouterView.

nodes.label: shouldShowChrome / meta.shell opt-in
nodes.rationale: Routes opt into the shell via route.meta.shell === true (and not popped-out windows), so un-migrated views render exactly as today — no double headers; allows the three phases to land independently.

nodes.label: useCommandPalette shared open state
nodes.rationale: Module-level open flag so the palette (mounted once in App.vue) and any opener (the rail's Search button, the Cmd+K handler) share one state.

nodes.label: ProjectTabNav (Issues/MRs/Pipelines tabs)
nodes.rationale: Three-way tab set with a running-pipeline count adornment driven by usePipelines + isActivePipeline; list views drop bespoke headers and teleport actions into #app-topbar-slot.

nodes.label: AppTopBar detail branch
nodes.rationale: Back + breadcrumb + #iid for issue/merge-request routes; the #app-topbar-slot carries the view's teleported actions.

nodes.label: ViewContainer 'bare' width
nodes.rationale: A passthrough (no max-width/centering/padding) so a view that sometimes renders inside an existing container (popped-out window's main, the drawer) can use the same ViewContainer wrapper unconditionally.

nodes.label: IssueDetail/IssueMasthead three modes
nodes.rationale: Full-page main window (shell present → masthead teleports actions, breadcrumb from shell), popped-out native window (?window=1 → no shell, masthead renders its own header), and embedded drawer (renders inline, no teleport). The teleport happens only in the !embedded && !windowed branch.

nodes.label: serverHealth host state machine
nodes.rationale: GitLab reachability becomes a single host-owned state machine (ok | down | expired) with one recovery loop and countdown, mirrored by every window — replacing per-window detection/recovery duplication. Probe fn and broadcaster are injected for testability.

nodes.label: observe() request-outcome feed
nodes.rationale: The host's gitlabGraphql/gitlabRest are the request choke point (every GitLab request flows through them), so each result's classification feeds observe(); classifyStatus mirrors the old probeServer rules including bodyless-403 = edge/LB block = down.

nodes.label: Window registry + lumen:server-health broadcast
nodes.rationale: There was no unified window registry (main win, issueWindows Map, settingsWindow tracked separately); a new registry lets state changes broadcast lumen:server-health to every window.

nodes.label: installServerHealth webview mirror
nodes.rationale: Thin replacement for installAuthWatch/probeServer/useServerRecovery: mirrors host state into sessionState and refetches this window's queries on recovery; banner/overlay become pure displays.

nodes.label: Confirm-then-latch auth rule
nodes.rationale: A single transient 401 must NOT expire the session: an auth observation is confirmed by a probe before latching expired; if the probe is clean the state stays ok.

nodes.label: In-process MCP server (Bun)
nodes.rationale: Opt-in, localhost-only (127.0.0.1), bearer-authenticated Bun.serve in the main process exposing GitLab tools to external agents; self-contained src/bun/mcp/ module.

nodes.label: Stateless streamable-HTTP transport per request
nodes.rationale: The SDK's WebStandardStreamableHTTPServerTransport verified to run under Bun in stateless JSON mode (sessionIdGenerator: undefined, enableJsonResponse: true) — a fresh McpServer + transport per request; Web-standard Request/Response makes the handler testable under Vitest without Bun.serve.

nodes.label: McpTool registry
nodes.rationale: Tools are plain objects { name, description, inputSchema (Zod raw shape), handler } collected in a registry and registered with the SDK, which auto-converts to JSON Schema and validates tools/call args.

nodes.label: GitLab MCP tool suites
nodes.rationale: Issues/MRs/labels-milestones/users-search tools hand-write GraphQL (no codegen) and call the existing gitlabGraphql/gitlabRest clients. Verified GitLab quirks: iid args are String; CreateIssueInput.labels takes title strings but most label/assignee/milestone inputs need GlobalIDs; approve/unapprove are REST-only; no cross-project search.

nodes.label: SettingsWindow two-pane native window
nodes.rationale: A singleton BrowserWindow mirroring the issue-window machinery loads the SPA at /settings?window=1 (shell chrome hidden); the route is handed over via rpc.getInitialRoute at boot, not baked into the URL. Replaces the single-column SettingsDialog modal, which is retired.

nodes.label: Agent access pane (MCP control)
nodes.rationale: Makes the MCP server fully controllable from settings via getMcpStatus/regenerateMcpToken/revealMcpToken RPCs; setMcpEnabled auto-generates a token when enabling without one.

nodes.label: Router guard settings allow-list
nodes.rationale: 'settings' is always reachable regardless of configured state because it hosts the Connection pane — it must open even when not yet configured.

nodes.label: IssueDrawer component
nodes.rationale: Right-anchored ~480px Sheet reusing IssueDetail unchanged in its body (it is already a self-contained props → useIssue unit); Expand routes to the existing full page.

nodes.label: Query-param drawer routing (?issue=iid)
nodes.rationale: Route-driven via ?issue= on the issues route so back button, refresh, and shareable links all work and the list (filters/scroll) stays mounted behind the drawer. Rejected: nested child route (router restructuring) and local state (not shareable). Close uses router.replace so it doesn't pollute history.

nodes.label: Lumen — personal GitLab issue tracker
nodes.rationale: Single-user, locally-run SPA over a self-hosted GitLab; GitLab remains the single source of truth — no local database, no deployed backend. Non-goals: multi-user auth, offline support, data mirroring.

nodes.label: Server-side token injection via dev-server proxy
nodes.rationale: The Vite proxy reads the PAT from .env server-side and attaches the auth header on every proxied request, so the token never ships in client bundles or browser storage.
nodes.norm_label: server-side token injection via dev-server proxy

nodes.label: TanStack Query + graphql-request split
nodes.rationale: TanStack Query owns caching/refetch/pagination/state; graphql-request only sends the query; codegen provides typed documents. Apollo/urql rejected because their normalized caches would duplicate Vue Query.

nodes.label: useScratchpad composable
nodes.rationale: localStorage-backed ref keyed lumen:scratchpad:${fullPath}#${iid} (mirrors the issueKey convention); re-keys when fullPath/iid change; empty content removes the entry. Private, never sent to GitLab.

nodes.label: Scratchpad component
nodes.rationale: Named Scratchpad to distinguish from GitLab 'Notes' (remote comments); auto-saves debounced ~500ms with a transient Saved hint, no save button; appears in both drawer and full page because the drawer embeds IssueDetail.

nodes.label: Spec: Assignee Editor + Quick Assign Split
nodes.norm_label: spec: assignee editor + quick assign split

nodes.label: assigneeSections pure helper
nodes.rationale: Derives current assignees + relationship-grouped sections (originator → assignees → commenters → members) from an issue + member list so AssigneeEditor and QuickAssign share one source of truth; issue accepted structurally to stay decoupled from generated types.

nodes.label: AssigneeEditor component
nodes.rationale: Stacked full-name rows with per-row remove plus an add/toggle dropdown that stays open for multi-toggle; every mutation recomputes the full username list and commits via useSetAssignees REPLACE semantics.

nodes.label: QuickAssign (simplified)
nodes.rationale: Separate labelled button; click performs a replace (sole assignee) and closes; per-assignee remove and unassign-all moved out into the editor.

nodes.label: Spec: Comments-in-Save, Grouped Labels, Collapsible Scratchpad
nodes.norm_label: spec: comments-in-save, grouped labels, collapsible scratchpad

nodes.label: Comments folded into Save/Cancel
nodes.rationale: A pending comment alone marks the issue dirty; the comment is a note (not an issue field) so it stays out of the pure IssueDraft/diffIssueEdit and is owned by the orchestration composable — yielding a single dirty/save/footer/guard.

nodes.label: labelGroups (groupLabelsByScope + toggleScoped)
nodes.rationale: Groups labels by scope:: prefix with preferred scopes first and unscoped 'Other' last; toggleScoped enforces scoped-label exclusivity (one value per scope) for add-label, while the filter keeps plain multi-toggle since filters can span scopes.

nodes.label: Collapsible scratchpad
nodes.rationale: Default collapsed with a content marker; open/closed state remembered per issue in localStorage; Textarea v-show-hidden to preserve value/focus.

nodes.label: IssueComposer slide-over
nodes.rationale: Replaces the always-visible title-only quick-create bar with an on-demand right-side Sheet (header button, C key, empty-state button) offering full-fidelity creation with progressive disclosure; deliberately mirrors IssueDrawer so reading and creating feel like one family of surfaces.

nodes.label: useProjectMembers composable
nodes.rationale: The app had no users query (assignees only known from loaded issues); mirrors useProjectLabels with projectMembers query, null filtering, normalizeError, ~5min staleTime.

nodes.label: Flash-highlight confirmation
nodes.rationale: Preserves the quiet no-toast feel: after the invalidated list refetches, briefly highlight the row whose iid matches the newly created issue, then fade — small transient highlightIid state on IssueList.

nodes.label: Spec: Issue Editing, Filtering & Editable Tags
nodes.norm_label: spec: issue editing, filtering & editable tags

nodes.label: issueEdit pure lib (IssueDraft, diffIssueEdit)
nodes.rationale: Pure draft/dirty/diff model: label diff computes add/removeLabelIds from id-set delta, state diff maps to CLOSE/REOPEN events, assignee diff is order-insensitive and emits the full next list because setAssignees uses REPLACE semantics.

nodes.label: useIssueDraft buffered-edit composable
nodes.rationale: Holds a reactive draft seeded from the issue and re-syncs from server refetches only while the buffer is clean — never clobbers in-flight edits. save() runs diffIssueEdit and fires the update/assignee mutations; Cancel reverts; tags are just a buffered field.

nodes.label: useIssueFilters URL-persisted filters
nodes.rationale: Single source of truth for state/search/labels/assignee/author serialized to the route query (search debounced), shared by list and board so both views filter identically; URL persistence matches the ?issue= drawer pattern.

nodes.label: ConfirmDialog dirty guard
nodes.rationale: Confirm-before-discard built on shadcn-vue alert-dialog, resolving a promise; applied via onBeforeRouteLeave on the full page and via the drawer close/iid-switch handlers, with dirty state surfaced from the embedded detail to its host.

nodes.label: QuickAssign Component
nodes.rationale: Deliberately separate from any fuller add/remove-assignee UI — this is the one-click reassign. Immediate commit via useUpdateIssue with invalidation; optimistic cache patching rejected as invalidation matches the existing detail-view mutation idiom.

nodes.label: orderAssignees Helper (assigneeOrder.ts)
nodes.rationale: Ordering uses comment authorship as a proxy because GitLab exposes no assignee-event endpoint in GraphQL or REST. Everything keyed by username (never id) because issue.author and note authors expose no id and the assigneeUsernames mutation input is username-based.

nodes.label: EditableField Edit/Preview Wrapper
nodes.rationale: Per-field independent toggles; rendered views read from the draft so Preview reflects in-progress unsaved edits. Fields return to rendered only on successful save (guard on !dirty) so unsaved edits aren't hidden behind a rendered view.

nodes.label: classifyUpload Media Classifier
nodes.rationale: GitLab embeds every uploaded attachment with image Markdown syntax regardless of type, so a .mp4 upload shows as a broken image; classification by extension fixes this — video/audio render native elements, non-media uploads render a download chip.

nodes.label: extractMedia Token Extraction
nodes.rationale: Runs the same Marked instance + gitlabImage tokenizer as rendering; rendering and extraction share classifyUpload + rewriteUploadSrc so they never drift.

nodes.label: MediaViewer Fullscreen Lightbox
nodes.rationale: Manual gallery (no auto-advance); audio and file chips are inline-only since audio has nothing to show and files just download. Source attribution distinguishes description vs comment media.

nodes.label: data-media-src Delegated Trigger Pattern
nodes.rationale: data-media-src carries the rewritten proxy URL so viewer and inline element load the identical resource. Videos keep inline controls (body click = play); a hover expand overlay carries the trigger so play isn't hijacked — one delegated click code path for images and videos.

nodes.label: Persist Issue-List View State Design Spec
nodes.norm_label: persist issue-list view state design spec

nodes.label: useIssueFilters URL-Backed View State
nodes.rationale: URL is the single source of truth; localStorage seeds the URL on arrival and mirrors it on change, keyed per project (lumen:issue-filters:<fullPath>). Saved state never overrides a URL that already specifies a filter key, so explicit/shared links win. Keys omitted at defaults keep links clean.

nodes.label: Bun RPC GitLab Transport
nodes.rationale: Replace exactly one thing — the Vite token-injecting dev-server proxy — so the Vue app changes as little as possible. Webview → typed RPC → Bun does the upstream fetch holding the token; the token never enters the webview. graphql-request kept with a custom fetch so no query call-site changes.

nodes.label: useGitlabAsset Blob-URL Asset Loading
nodes.rationale: RPC → blob URL chosen because it uses only confirmed Electrobun APIs; a custom-scheme handler is a later simplification if the API proves clean. Memoized per path with revoke on unmount.

nodes.label: Disk-Persisted Query Cache
nodes.rationale: localStorage persister via @tanstack/query-persist-client-core: native webview localStorage is disk-backed per app so cache survives restart and renders instantly offline. buster keyed on gitlabUrl + token fingerprint so switching instances or rotating the token invalidates stale cache.

nodes.label: SettingsDialog Modal
nodes.rationale: Centered modal dialog chosen over a settings page because the app has no persistent chrome to navigate from and the dialog keeps the user in their work context. No invented preference rows — all persisted state is per-project and the theme is deliberately fixed.

nodes.label: useSettings Singleton + Menu Bridge
nodes.rationale: macOS app menu is the idiomatic entry point since the shell renders no persistent header; host→webview bridge via executeJavascript dispatching the lumen:open-settings CustomEvent, with a webview ⌘, onKeyStroke fallback in case the menu bridge regresses.

nodes.label: useGitlabConnect Shared Connect Logic
nodes.rationale: Extracted from onboarding so the swap-token form is not a copy of onboarding: the composable owns the probe; callers decide the success side-effect (navigate vs toast).

nodes.label: clearPersistedCache Helper
nodes.rationale: Explicit key on createSyncStoragePersister (lumen:query-cache) so the same constant is used to write and to clear — don't rely on the library default key string.

nodes.label: Feature-Sliced Structure
nodes.rationale: Domain is the top split; big-bang reorg via git mv preserves history. Verification gate is the vitest suite, not typecheck, because src/gitlab/generated is gitignored and stays red until a user-run bun codegen. No per-feature barrel files to avoid added indirection; god-file splits deliberately deferred to keep the diff a pure move.

nodes.label: Issue Multi-Select Bulk + Combined Window Design Spec
nodes.norm_label: issue multi-select bulk + combined window design spec

nodes.label: useIssueSelection Composable
nodes.rationale: Ephemeral selection state shared via provide/inject (Symbol key) to avoid prop-threading through v-for; the Set survives filtering/sort/pagination; rows/cards fall back to a no-op state when not provided so isolated tests render unchanged.

nodes.label: BulkActionBar Component
nodes.rationale: The bar holds no mutation logic — it emits intent that IssueList or the bulk composable handles. Selection is kept after a successful bulk action so the user can chain actions.

nodes.label: useBulkIssueActions Composable
nodes.rationale: A bulk apply is N independent mutations reusing the existing optimistic per-issue mutations (useRetagIssue/useReassignIssue/useSetIssueStatus) via Promise.allSettled; failures are surfaced in a result toast but do not roll back successes. Confirm dialog first.

nodes.label: MultiIssueWindow Pager View
nodes.rationale: Single issue at a time behind a '‹ X of N ›' pager, each page fully editable by reusing IssueDetail in windowed mode keyed by current iid so paging remounts cleanly; paging away from a dirty page prompts to discard. Spawns a new window each time (no dedupe), URL built by issuesWindowUrl.

nodes.label: Open Issue in Native Window Design Spec
nodes.norm_label: open issue in native window design spec

nodes.label: openIssueWindow RPC + Window Registry
nodes.rationale: One window per issue: a module-level Map keyed by fullPath#iid means re-expanding an already-open issue focuses its existing window rather than spawning a duplicate; frame cascades by registry size so stacked windows don't fully overlap. Each window gets a fresh buildRpc() instance.

nodes.label: issueWindowUrl URL Builder
nodes.rationale: Pure and testable, matching the existing startUrl.ts/menu.ts testable-helper pattern; works for both HMR and bundled views:// bases.

nodes.label: IssueDetail Windowed Mode (?window=1)
nodes.rationale: The issue window is a focused single-issue view, so the back-to-list arrow is suppressed; useTitle and the unsaved-edits route-leave guard stay enabled — both correct for a standalone window. Each window cold-boots the whole SPA with no shared query cache, acceptable for a daily-driver desktop app.

nodes.label: useSession installAuthWatch
nodes.rationale: A single watcher subscribed to both the vue-query query and mutation caches flips sessionState.expired on any GitLabError kind 'auth' — global detection with no per-view changes. The host is stateless w.r.t. the token (loadConfig reads fresh from disk per RPC), so no host relaunch is needed: the original 'restart the backend' framing was a misread of the architecture.

nodes.label: SessionExpiredOverlay
nodes.rationale: Non-dismissable full-screen blocker — the only exits are a successful reconnect or an explicit disconnect; reuses useGitlabConnect for the in-place token re-entry.

nodes.label: Recovery via Full Webview Reload
nodes.rationale: Full reload chosen over in-place invalidateQueries (edge cases with queries stuck in error) and over redirecting to ConnectView (framed as onboarding, loses location). The recovery probe goes through rpc.gitlabGraphql directly, bypassing vue-query, so a 401 while testing a fresh token never reaches the cache watcher — no suppression flag needed, the separation is structural.

nodes.label: useRepoPath Shared Composable
nodes.rationale: Replaces the pathParts/repoName/pathPrefix trio copy-pasted across IssueList, IssueDetail, and PipelineList.

nodes.label: useTabNav Shared Composable
nodes.rationale: onTabNav modifier-aware View-Transition route hop, deduplicated from IssueList and PipelineList.

nodes.label: useSpringCursor Shared Composable
nodes.rationale: Critically-damped selection-rail spring with no domain knowledge, extracted from ProjectPicker. Existing view tests are the regression gate and must pass unchanged — behavior-preserving refactor to roughly ≤250 lines per view.

nodes.label: useGroupOrder Persistence Store
nodes.rationale: Groups/columns are synthetic — built client-side from labels and work-item statuses; GitLab has no API to persist an order for them, so custom order is a local per-project presentation preference. Indexing by the dimension string makes the order shared across list and board for free. Not captured by Saved Views to avoid coupling and snapshot bloat.

nodes.label: applyOrder Pure Reorder Pass
nodes.rationale: Stable reordering: groups in the stored order come first, unknown groups keep default relative order and append after, stale keys are ignored — so new columns land at the end and deleted columns are harmless without special-casing '__none'.

nodes.label: useGroupReorder Drag Interaction (HTML5 DnD)
nodes.rationale: Native HTML5 drag from a dedicated header grip with a custom dataTransfer type (application/x-lumen-group) to stay distinct from card drags; the codebase hand-rolls DnD throughout, so no new drag library. Card DnD handlers already early-return when no card is in hand, so there is no collision.

nodes.label: Pointer-Events Reorder Mechanism
nodes.rationale: Native HTML5 DnD structurally caps ghost/indicator quality — a static drag image, no live insertion line, no edge auto-scroll — so the reorder interaction is rewritten on pointer events for a Linear/Notion-class feel (cursor-following chip, live insertion bar, lift/settle, edge auto-scroll). Card drag-and-drop stays native and untouched; all motion respects prefers-reduced-motion.

nodes.label: insertionIndexFor Pure Geometry
nodes.rationale: All inputs are plain data so it unit-tests with synthetic DOMRects on both axes; items are discovered via data-reorder-key attributes so the composable never holds Vue refs.

nodes.label: reorderToIndex Commit Helper
nodes.rationale: Index-based commit replaces the old over-key-based reorderKeys, which is removed along with its tests since the pointer flow leaves it with no caller.

nodes.label: 'unavailable' Error Kind Taxonomy
nodes.rationale: A user must never be prompted to enter a new token when the server is merely unavailable — the token prompt is reserved for genuine auth failures. Thrown fetch (DNS/refused/timeout) and 5xx map to 'unavailable'; 401/403 stay 'auth'; the host layer must preserve the transport-throw vs HTTP-status signal.

nodes.label: ConnectionBanner Non-Blocking Banner
nodes.rationale: Non-blocking, self-healing: no token field, no manual dismiss; it clears itself when the recovery poll succeeds.

nodes.label: Active Recovery Poll with Backoff
nodes.rationale: Polls the cheap currentUser health query on 2s → 5s → 15s backoff only while unavailable. Auth always wins: a poll 401/403 escalates unavailable → expired, and auth state never downgrades to unavailable.

nodes.label: Palette Command Sources (sources.ts)
nodes.rationale: The single ~120-line commands computed already mixes route logic, project search, and #-jump; adding issue search + views inline would make it unmaintainable. Typed pure source builders with PaletteContext passed in (not reached for) give clean boundaries and test seams.

nodes.label: usePaletteCommands Merger
nodes.rationale: Merges sources into a grouped, ranked list with fixed group order; flat keyboard nav walks a single flattened index spanning all groups.

nodes.label: usePaletteIssueSearch Debounced Search
nodes.rationale: Follows the manual-type pattern of useIssue.ts (inline template-literal document + hand-written result type) so typecheck stays green without running bun codegen. Debounced ~200ms, gated on ≥2 chars / not a pure #number / project open; search failure renders zero hits silently and never blocks other groups.

nodes.label: Issue Details Rail Progressive Disclosure Design Spec
nodes.norm_label: issue details rail progressive disclosure design spec

nodes.label: RAIL_FIELDS Descriptor Registry
nodes.rationale: Metadata-only registry: the rail's fields render heterogeneous bespoke markup, and forcing them behind one uniform component contract is not worth it — the registry owns visibility/ordering/menu/removal, rendering stays in the template. A future field = one RAIL_FIELDS entry + one template block. Built to scale to the full GitLab attribute set rather than hand-maintained per-field branches.

nodes.label: useRailFields Visibility Composable
nodes.rationale: revealed/removed are transient this-session intent, cleared on save and cancel only — nothing new persists to GitLab or localStorage.

nodes.label: Rail Field Visibility State Machine
nodes.rationale: Visibility is fully derived from four inputs (original populated, draft populated, revealed set, removed set) — no per-field UI flags scattered around the component. Pinned fields (Status, Labels, Assignees) anchor every issue and never enter the Add menu; booleans ride the same model with isPopulated = flag===true.

nodes.label: Merge Requests Browse + Triage Design Spec
nodes.norm_label: merge requests browse + triage design spec

nodes.label: features/merge_requests Module
nodes.rationale: Mirrors the proven features/issues patterns but lean — answers the daily 'what MRs need my attention' loop without the heavy diff-review machinery. Manual-typed GraphQL (no codegen); the MR detail discussion shape matches useIssue so IssueDiscussion rendering is reused as-is. labelName used because the labels arg is deprecated upstream.

nodes.label: Generalized useSavedViews (namespace + keys)
nodes.rationale: The one shared lift — the only change to existing stable code: parameterize storage namespace + filter keys so issue and MR saved views live in separate localStorage namespaces while preserving existing issue behavior and tests.

nodes.label: useMrFilters Composable
nodes.rationale: Mirrors useIssueFilters: URL-encoded reactive slice, restored from localStorage per project; the pure slice → GraphQL-args mapping lives in lib/mrView.ts so it is unit-testable without mounting.

nodes.label: MR Palette Integration (!iid jump + MR group)
nodes.rationale: Uses the !iid convention to avoid colliding with the issue #123 jump; reuses the issue search's gcTime:0 + persist.ts dehydrate-exclusion treatment.
nodes.norm_label: mr palette integration (!iid jump + mr group)

nodes.label: In-App MCP Server (src/bun/mcp)
nodes.rationale: Lives in the Bun main process because it already holds the GitLab credentials and client, so GitLab tools are a direct reuse. SDK Server + StreamableHTTPServerTransport chosen over a hand-rolled protocol (protocol/validation risk) and over proxying into the webview (couples to a live webview and reactive composables). HTTP fits a long-running GUI; stdio deferred.

nodes.label: MCP Tool Registry
nodes.rationale: ToolDef { name, description, inputSchema, handler }; lumen_<domain>_<verb> naming; the registry answers tools/list and dispatches tools/call with validated args. Destructive MR merge intentionally excluded in v1.

nodes.label: MCP Auth Gate (127.0.0.1 + Bearer)
nodes.rationale: Safe by default: off by default, opt-in via Settings, localhost-only bind, generated bearer token rejected with 401 before the request reaches the SDK; port-in-use surfaces an error rather than silently roaming.

nodes.label: App-Control Bridge (reportAppState + command push)
nodes.rationale: Built from the two proven RPC directions — no new framework capability: the webview pushes state snapshots via a reportAppState handler (read tools return the cache instantly, no synchronous round-trip), and the host drives the webview via executeJavascript dispatching lumen:mcp-command CustomEvents. Drives are fire-and-forget; the agent re-reads lumen_app_state to confirm.

nodes.label: My Work Dashboard View
nodes.rationale: Opening the app lands on a cross-project home answering 'what needs me right now': assigned issues, assigned MRs, review-requested MRs as three lanes. Project Picker moves to /projects with route name 'projects' preserved so every existing push keeps working unchanged. The first:25 query cap is the view — no pagination in v1.

nodes.label: DashboardLane Component
nodes.rationale: Per-lane independence: each lane binds its own query's isLoading/error/data, so one lane failing or loading never blanks the others; inbox-zero still shows the three headers so the page doesn't look broken.

nodes.label: parseIssuePath Helper
nodes.rationale: The root issues query's Issue type has no project object (only webPath/webUrl/projectId), so fullPath+iid are parsed from webPath with nested-group support; on parse failure the row falls back to the external webUrl.

nodes.label: AppShell Persistent Chrome
nodes.rationale: Replaces ad-hoc duplicated per-view headers with one persistent shell: slim global icon rail + contextual top bar + standard content container. Context derivation needs no new store — the top bar and tabs are pure functions of the route. Views feed affordances into named shell slots.

nodes.label: Chrome-Off Rule
nodes.rationale: connect, issues-window, and any ?window=1 route render the view WITHOUT the shell in the same minimal main container as today, so every native-window flow is untouched. Static cases via route.meta.chrome === false; the window query checked live.

nodes.label: ProjectTabNav Component
nodes.rationale: Issues/MRs/Pipelines tabs for a fullPath; carries the running-pipeline adornment that previously lived in the deleted per-view headers.

nodes.label: ViewContainer Width Container
nodes.rationale: Width stops being re-decided per view: default max-w-5xl with a small width-prop enum (wide for board/picker, narrow for My Work); every view's ad-hoc outer wrapper is deleted.

nodes.label: serverHealth Host State Machine
nodes.rationale: With N windows there were N independent recovery loops with visibly drifting countdowns. The Bun host already proxies every GitLab request, so it is the natural choke point: passive detection classifies every request outcome (no webview 'report down' RPC), one recovery loop (backoff 2/5/15 + 1s countdown), and broadcast to all windows. The probe must not re-enter observe() — no feedback loop. Takes probe fn + broadcaster as collaborators so it unit-tests without electrobun.

nodes.label: Confirm-Then-Latch Auth Rule
nodes.rationale: A single transient 401 must not log the user out: an auth outcome while ok triggers a cooldown-guarded confirm probe, and only a confirmed failure latches 'expired'. These semantics are well-tested in useSession today and must be ported to serverHealth.observe with equivalent tests, not dropped.

nodes.label: Window Registry + lumen:server-health Broadcast
nodes.rationale: A unified Set of all BrowserWindows; broadcast iterates and executeJavascripts a CustomEvent dispatch into each. Broadcasting to a not-yet-ready webview is harmless (no listener); the getServerHealth boot pull seeds late-joiners.

nodes.label: installServerHealth Webview Mirror
nodes.rationale: Windows become thin displays: sessionState is written ONLY by the health listener (no local detection); installAuthWatch, probeServer, and the useServerRecovery probe loop are removed. On a down→ok transition each window invalidates its own queries since each window owns its own cache.

nodes.label: SettingsWindow Two-Pane Native Window
nodes.rationale: Two-pane sidebar in a dedicated native window chosen over a roomier modal or full-screen route; seven panes, every pane backs a real feature (Notifications included only after verifying usePipelineNotifications is a real source). Each pane is a focused unit reading its config slice and saving via one RPC; panes do not know about each other. No cross-window reactive state — each window does its own RPC.

nodes.label: Agent Access (MCP) Pane
nodes.rationale: Makes the MCP server fully usable from the UI (enable/port/token reveal/copy/regenerate, client-config snippet, live status) — no more hand-editing config.json. The bearer token is returned only by explicit reveal/regenerate RPCs, never in getConfig/getMcpStatus. Disconnect also stops the MCP server, folding in the deferred clearConfig → stopMcp fix.

nodes.label: openSettingsWindow Singleton Opener
nodes.rationale: Module-level singleton ref (like issueWindows but single): a second open focuses the existing window instead of spawning another; the ⌘, menu item and lumen:open-settings event both route here, retiring the modal.
